topic_title: Matrices — grids of numbers and how AI uses them
verdict: PASS — both hard findings from cycle 1 are resolved. (1) Section 9 optional paragraph (TensorFlow Embedding Projector / word representations / semantic clustering) has been removed; the hands-on exercise is now a self-contained matrix-building task within scope. (2) Section 5.5 weight-matrix sub-section trimmed to 2 sentences: names the weight matrix, states what it holds, defers matrix multiplication to a later topic — within the prescribed 1–2 sentence depth limit. No hard findings remain. Three soft findings carried forward (non-blocking, surface to HITL #2/#3): (a) sequence_drift soft — 'neural network', 'layer', 'neurons' in section 7 are not in available prior concepts 6.1–6.3; they function as contextual scaffolding in a real-world-patterns example and were soft in cycle 1; (b) scope_creep soft — line 96 colour-image mention explains the RGB three-grid structure ('one grid for red, one for green, one for blue') rather than bare-naming and deferring; trim to 'colour images are covered in a later topic' if the builder wants to be strict; (c) beginner_readability soft — 'neural network' and 'neuron' (2 terms) used without prior definition in this sequence; at the ≤2 threshold so stays soft, not escalated.

length_sanity.detail: Estimated ~1570 words (progress log recorded 1580w; section 9 optional paragraph removed so slightly lower). Within the standard corpus range of 1500–6000 words used in cycle 1 for this topic. Word count is at the low end of the standard profile; not more than 30% outside the lower bound.

scope_creep_findings.detail: Text reads: 'Colour images add a third dimension — one grid for red, one for green, one for blue — but that idea is covered in a later topic.' The scope_discipline rule requires bare name + topic pointer only; this sentence explains the RGB structure (three grids, one per channel) rather than just naming and deferring. The defer pointer is present, which limits the impact. Suggested fix: replace with 'Colour images extend this idea further — that is covered in a later topic.' New finding in cycle 2 (was not present in prior corpus version).

hard_scope_creep_resolved: TensorFlow Embedding Projector / word representations / semantic clustering (section 9 optional paragraph) — REMOVED in cycle 2
Section 5.5 weight-matrix depth exceeding one-sentence limit — TRIMMED to 2 sentences in cycle 2

soft_findings.detail: 'Neural network' used parenthetically in section 5.5 and as a section header in section 7 without prior definition in this sequence (6.1–6.3 do not introduce it). 'Neuron' appears in section 7 ('128 neurons') without definition. Two terms — at the ≤2 threshold; stays soft, not escalated to hard. The previous cycle-1 jargon-density finding in section 5.5 is resolved: trimmed to 2 sentences with no stacked undefined terms.

beginner_readability.detail: Introduction and Core Concepts prose is plain, active voice, addresses learner as 'you'. All non-everyday terms (matrix, row, column, cell, weight matrix) are defined on first use with bold-term + plain-English format. No unexplained acronyms. No hedging phrases. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms at once. Only 1 distinct soft finding — below the ≥3 threshold for hard escalation. Readability is strong overall.

soft_findings_summary: sequence_drift (soft): 'neural network', 'layer', 'neurons' in section 7 — contextual scaffolding for weight matrices, not in prior concepts 6.1–6.3; carried from cycle 1
scope_creep (soft): colour image RGB explanation in section 5.4 (~line 96) explains three-grid structure rather than bare-naming and deferring — minor but violates scope_discipline form rule
beginner_readability (soft): 'neural network' and 'neuron' (2 terms) used without prior definition in sequence — at ≤2 threshold, non-escalated
recommended_fixes: Section 5.4 (~line 96): Replace 'Colour images add a third dimension — one grid for red, one for green, one for blue — but that idea is covered in a later topic.' with 'Colour images extend this idea — that is covered in a later topic.' to conform to the bare-name-and-defer form rule.

Section 7 neural network example: Consider adding a one-word gloss for 'neuron' (e.g. 'neuron — a processing unit') to satisfy the define-on-first-use mandate for beginner learners; or remove the '128 neurons' specificity and state '128 processing units' without the technical term.
